Typical price range for a rebuilt unit with 12-month warranty is £3,200-£10,500 - supply & fit available nationwide.

engineGuide.h2: BMW M2 Engine Replacement - All Variants Covered

entries.title: 3.0 Litre Petrol
entries.history: The first-generation M2's turbocharged straight-six, derived from the N55 in the M235i but featuring forged components, upgraded cooling, and a dedicated M-specific tune.
commonFailures: Charge pipe failure (brittle plastic cracks under boost pressure - common from 30,000 miles)
Crank hub spin (less common than S55 but documented on tuned examples)
entries.cta: Get quotes for N55B30T0 BMW M2 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0 Litre Petrol
entries.history: A high-performance derivative of the N55 developed by BMW M GmbH, featuring a closed-deck block, twin turbos, and a water-to-air charge cooling system. Fitted to the M2 Competition as a detuned version of the unit from the F80 M3 and F82 M4.
commonFailures: Crank hub spin (well-documented failure where the hub rotates on the crankshaft, causing valve timing drift - affects both standard and tuned engines)
Oil consumption (some units require piston ring replacement)
entries.cta: Get quotes for S55B30A BMW M2 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0 Litre Petrol
entries.history: The highest-output factory tune of the S55 used in the limited-production M2 CS, sharing its calibration with the M4 Competition package.
commonFailures: Crank hub spin (same vulnerability as the Competition variant)
entries.cta: Get quotes for S55B30T0 BMW M2 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0 Litre Petrol
entries.history: BMW M's twin-turbocharged straight-six that powers the second-generation G87 M2. Features a closed-deck block, forged crankshaft, and 3D-printed cylinder head core. BMW resolved the crank hub vulnerability from the S55 with a single-piece hub design.
commonFailures: Oil consumption (some early G87 units reported higher than expected usage)
Crank hub issues resolved (unlike S55) - no systematic failures documented
Tappet noise on cold start (characteristic but not a failure)
entries.cta: Get quotes for S58B30T0 BMW M2 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0 Litre Petrol
entries.history: A higher-output evolution of the S58 platform anticipated for future G87 M2 CS and Competition variants, featuring revised turbocharger mapping and increased boost pressure.
entries.cta: Get quotes for S58B30A BMW M2 engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: BMW M2 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The F87 and G87 M2 are defined by their turbocharged straight-six engines - the N55, S55, and S58. While fundamentally robust, each has well-documented failure points that can lead to significant repair bills or full replacement. Below, we break down the most common BMW M2 engine problems by engine family, with real UK costs for dealer vs specialist repairs, and honest advice on when a rebuilt engine with upgraded internals offers the best value. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.group: BMW M2 S55 Crank Hub Failure
problems.h4: BMW M2 S55 Crank Hub Failure - The Achilles' Heel of the Competition & CS
problems.affectedModels: BMW M2 Competition, M2 CS (F87, 2018-2021, S55B30A & S55B30T0)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 20,000 - 60,000 miles (can occur on stock or tuned engines)
problems.rootCause: The crank hub is attached to the crankshaft via friction alone, not a keyway. Sudden load changes - aggressive downshifts or high-rpm launches - can cause the hub to spin on the crank, jumping timing and allowing valves to contact pistons.

repairOptions.tier: Repair Tier
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: What It Involves
repairOptions.longevity: Longevity / Suitability

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Crank Hub Fix - Capture hub + bolt upgrade
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit aftermarket pinned or captured hub design without removing engine
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent prevention if done proactively. Best for tuned or track-used cars

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Timing Correction & Hub Replacement - Post-spin (no piston contact)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove oil pan, timing components, replace hub, re-time engine
repairOptions.longevity: Successful only if spin detected immediately and no valve contact occurred

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt S55 Engine - With upgraded hub
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit fully rebuilt S55 with captured/pinned hub, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent solution. Required if valves bent or cylinder damage present

problems.recommendation: If your M2 Competition or CS is still running fine, proactively fitting a crank hub capture kit is excellent value at around £2,000 from a specialist. If the hub has already spun, have a borescope inspection performed immediately. If valves hit pistons, skip the intermediate repair - a rebuilt engine with an upgraded hub is the only reliable long-term fix. On an M2 worth £25,000-£45,000, £7,500-£10,000 for a properly rebuilt S55 makes financial sense.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt S55B30A engine prices for your BMW M2 Competition

problems.group: BMW M2 N55 Charge Pipe Failure
problems.h4: BMW M2 N55 Charge Pipe Failure - The Most Common Early M2 Problem
problems.rootCause: The factory plastic charge pipe between the intercooler and throttle body becomes brittle from heat cycling and cracks under boost pressure, creating a large boost leak and triggering limp mode.

repairOptions.tier: Repair Tier
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: What It Involves
repairOptions.longevity: Longevity / Suitability

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Replacement with OEM Plastic Pipe
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit new BMW plastic charge pipe
repairOptions.longevity: Will fail again. Only suitable for a quick sale, not long-term ownership

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Upgraded Aluminium Charge Pipe - Preventative
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit aftermarket aluminium pipe (e.g., VRSF, Evolution Racewerks)
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent fix. Does not address any existing engine damage

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt N55 Engine - Only if catastrophic failure occurred
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt N55 with upgraded charge pipe, warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Required only if the driver continued driving in limp mode causing overheating or turbo damage

problems.recommendation: Do not waste money on an OEM plastic replacement. An aluminium charge pipe costs £100-£200 in parts plus 2-3 hours labour - around £400-£550 total from a specialist. Fit one proactively before the factory pipe fails. If your M2 has already entered limp mode, clear the codes, fit the aluminium pipe, and you'll almost certainly be fine. The engine itself is robust.
problems.cta: Get quotes for N55B30T0 engine replacement and charge pipe upgrades

problems.h4: BMW M2 S55 Oil Consumption - Piston Ring & Valve Stem Seal Wear
problems.affectedModels: BMW M2 Competition, M2 CS (F87, 2018-2021, S55B30A & S55B30T0)
problems.rootCause: High cylinder pressures and heat cause piston rings to carbon-seize or valve stem seals to harden, allowing oil past the sealing surfaces. BMW considered 1 litre per 1,000 miles "acceptable" - many owners disagree.

repairOptions.tier: Repair Tier
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: What It Involves
repairOptions.longevity: Longevity / Suitability

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Oil Catch Can & Decarbonisation - Management
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit oil catch can, walnut blast intake valves
repairOptions.longevity: Reduces symptoms but does not fix root cause. Buy time for planning

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Valve Stem Seal Replacement - Heads-on
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace valve stem seals without full engine removal
repairOptions.longevity: Fixes blue smoke on startup. Does not address ring-related consumption under load

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt S55 with Upgraded Rings - Permanent fix
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit fully rebuilt S55 with low-tension rings, upgraded seals, bench tested
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent solution. Also allows crank hub upgrade during rebuild

problems.recommendation: Start with an oil consumption test to determine whether it's rings (consumption under load) or valve seals (smoke on startup). If you're burning more than 500ml per 1,000 miles, a full rebuild with upgraded rings is the only permanent fix. At 60,000+ miles on a Competition, budgeting for a rebuilt engine at £7,000-£9,000 is realistic planning, not panic.
problems.cta: Compare S55 rebuilt engine prices with upgraded piston rings

problems.group: BMW M2 N55 VANOS Solenoid & Cam Timing Issues
problems.h4: BMW M2 N55 VANOS Solenoid & Cam Timing Issues
problems.rootCause: VANOS solenoids become clogged with oil debris or wear electrically, causing inconsistent cam timing, rough idle, reduced power, and potentially limp mode with "VANOS" fault codes.

repairOptions.tier: Repair Tier
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: What It Involves
repairOptions.longevity: Longevity / Suitability

repairOptions.tier: Minor - VANOS Solenoid Cleaning & Oil Service
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove, clean, and test solenoids + fresh oil change
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix (6-12 months). Good diagnostic step

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Replacement VANOS Solenoids
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit new OEM or upgraded solenoids + oil service
repairOptions.longevity: Lasts 50,000+ miles. Most common permanent fix

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt N55 Engine - Only if timing chain damaged
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt N55 with new timing components
repairOptions.longevity: Required only if VANOS failure led to chain guide breakage or valve contact

problems.recommendation: At the first VANOS code, have a specialist clean the solenoids and inspect oil quality. If codes return within 1,000 miles, replace both solenoids - around £600 from an independent. This is not an engine-ending problem if caught early. Ignoring it can lead to timing chain strain and, rarely, catastrophic failure. On an early M2 worth £18,000-£25,000, the £500-£800 intermediate fix is the obvious choice.
problems.cta: Get N55 VANOS repair quotes or compare full engine replacement costs

problems.group: BMW M2 G87 S58 Early Reliability
problems.h4: BMW M2 G87 S58 Early Reliability - What We Know So Far
problems.rootCause: The S58 uses a single-piece crank hub (resolving the S55's weakness), but early examples show occasional oil consumption between services and cold-start tappet noise that owners mistake for failure.

repairOptions.tier: Repair Tier
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: What It Involves
repairOptions.longevity: Longevity / Suitability

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Oil Consumption Monitoring & Top-Up
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Regular oil level checks, top-up between services
repairOptions.longevity: Ongoing management. Most G87s do not require mechanical intervention

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Piston Ring Replacement - Under warranty if flagged early
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove sump, replace rings on affected cylinders
repairOptions.longevity: Rare - only for documented high oil consumption (1L/1,000 miles)

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt S58 Engine - Warranty covers most cases
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt S58 with factory-spec components
repairOptions.longevity: Very rare. G87 M2s are new; most failures will be warranty claims, not customer-pay rebuilds

problems.recommendation: If you own a G87 M2 consuming oil, document every top-up and request a consumption test from BMW. The S58 is proving more reliable than the S55, but it's still young. For owners planning to keep their G87 beyond warranty, a preventative crank hub upgrade is unnecessary - BMW fixed that design flaw. Stick to diligent oil changes every 5,000-6,000 miles rather than BMW's 12,000-mile interval.
problems.cta: Compare G87 M2 S58 engine replacement prices (warranty or customer-pay)

problems.group: BMW M2 N55 Boost Leak & Turbocharger Wear
problems.h4: BMW M2 N55 Boost Leak & Turbocharger Wear
problems.rootCause: The single twin-scroll turbocharger experiences wastegate bushing wear, leading to rattle, boost oscillation, and ultimately underboost codes. Vacuum lines and boost solenoids also degrade.

repairOptions.tier: Repair Tier
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: What It Involves
repairOptions.longevity: Longevity / Suitability

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Boost Solenoid & Vacuum Line Replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace vacuum lines, boost solenoids, pressure converters
repairOptions.longevity: Fixes boost control issues if turbo itself is healthy

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Turbocharger Rebuild - Core exchange
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove turbo, fit rebuilt cartridge with new wastegate bushings
repairOptions.longevity: Turbo functions like new. 12-month warranty typical

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt N55 with Upgraded Turbo
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt N55 with new or upgraded turbocharger
repairOptions.longevity: Required if turbo failure contaminated oil system with debris

problems.recommendation: If you hear wastegate rattle but boost is still stable, plan for a turbo rebuild within 10,000-20,000 miles. A specialist rebuild at £1,400-£1,800 is significantly cheaper than a new OEM turbo (£2,500+). If the turbo has already failed and sent debris through the engine, a full rebuilt engine with a new turbo is the only safe path - contaminated oil will kill the bottom end within months.
problems.cta: Compare N55 turbo rebuild and engine replacement quotes for BMW M2

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your BMW M2
finalCta.paragraph: From crank hub failures on S55-powered Competition and CS models to charge pipe issues on early N55 cars, M2 engine problems are predictable and manageable - if you know the costs and act early. EngineMarket connects you with trusted UK BMW M2 specialists who supply rebuilt engines with upgraded components designed to eliminate the original failure points. Whether you need a preventative crank hub fix, a turbo rebuild, or a complete S55 or N55 replacement with warranty, compare prices from our network and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.

faq.h2: BMW M2 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a BMW M2 engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A BMW M2 replacement engine typically costs £3,200-£10,500 supply-only, depending on engine code (N55, S55 or S58) and condition type.
keyPoints: S55 M2 Competition (2018-2021): rebuilt units £4,500-£8,500
S58 G87 M2 (2023-present): rebuilt units £5,500-£10,500
Add £600-£1,200 for specialist supply and fit nationwide
items.cta: Compare BMW M2 engine replacement prices from UK specialists

items.question: Is the BMW M2 S55 crank hub failure really that common?
items.answer: Yes - the S55 crank hub spin is the most documented failure on M2 Competition and CS models, affecting both stock and tuned cars between 20,000-60,000 miles .
keyPoints: OE design lacks a physical keyway - hub held by friction only
Aggressive downshifts or high-rpm launches can cause timing slip
Preventative pinned or captured hub fix costs £1,800-£2,500 fitted
Post-failure repair often requires full engine replacement at £7,500+
G87 S58 engine uses redesigned single-piece hub - issue resolved
items.cta: Get quotes for S55 crank hub preventative fix or rebuilt engine

items.question: What are the most common BMW M2 N55 engine problems?
items.answer: On the 2016-2018 N55-powered M2, the most common failures are cracked charge pipes (sudden boost loss and limp mode), ignition coil misfires, and oil leaks from ageing gaskets .
keyPoints: Plastic charge pipe cracks under boost - upgraded aluminium pipe is permanent fix
Ignition coil failure causes hesitation and rough idle - £170 average replacement
Valve cover and oil filter housing gaskets leak - £200-£350 repair
Electric water pump failure can cause overheating - £327 average replacement
VANOS solenoid wear affects cam timing on higher-mileage units
items.cta: Compare N55 M2 repair costs or replacement engine prices

items.question: Which BMW M2 engine is most reliable - N55, S55 or S58?
items.answer: The S58 in the G87 M2 is mechanically the most robust, having resolved the S55's crank hub vulnerability. The N55 is reliable with one cheap preventative fix, while the S55 needs a crank hub upgrade for peace of mind .
keyPoints: S58 (G87): single-piece crank hub, closed-deck block, excellent tuning potential
N55 (F87): reliable if charge pipe upgraded to aluminium (£400-£550)
S55 (Competition/CS): fundamentally strong but crank hub fix is essential
All three engines regularly exceed 100,000 miles with proper maintenance
G87 still too new for long-term failure data, early signs positive
items.cta: Compare rebuilt prices across N55, S55 and S58 BMW M2 engines

items.question: Can I tune my BMW M2 engine safely, and what are the risks?
items.answer: Yes - all three M2 engines respond well to tuning, but the S55 requires a crank hub fix before adding power. The S58 offers substantial gains with proper supporting mods .
keyPoints: N55: stage 1 remap safe with aluminium charge pipe fitted
S55: DO NOT tune without pinned crank hub - stock cars already spin hubs
S58: highly tunable - 500-600+ hp achievable with software and bolt-ons
Always use a specialist BMW tuner, not a generic remap
Factor in upgraded cooling for track use
items.cta: Get quotes for tuned BMW M2 engine builds and supporting modifications

items.question: How long does a BMW M2 engine last before needing replacement?
items.answer: A well-maintained BMW M2 engine typically lasts 100,000-150,000 miles. The N55 and S55 need preventative fixes around 50,000-80,000 miles to reach higher mileage reliably .
keyPoints: N55: charge pipe upgrade by 60,000 miles prevents cascade failures
S55: crank hub fix recommended by 50,000 miles or before tuning
Regular oil changes every 5,000-6,000 miles (not BMW's 12,000-mile interval)
Track use significantly accelerates wear - more frequent servicing required
Many F87 M2s now exceeding 80,000 miles on original engines
items.cta: Find a high-mileage BMW M2 replacement engine or rebuild option

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in an older BMW M2, or should I sell?
items.answer: On an F87 M2 worth £18,000-£45,000, spending £4,000-£9,000 on a rebuilt engine with upgraded components is financially sensible compared to selling with a failed engine.
keyPoints: Car with failed engine loses 40-60% of market value
Rebuilt N55: £3,200-£6,800 - adds 80,000+ miles of life
Rebuilt S55 with crank hub fix: £4,500-£8,500 - eliminates factory weakness
Specialist rebuild includes upgraded components and 12-24 month warranty
Lower cost than £12,000-£16,000 dealer replacement or buying another M2
items.cta: Compare rebuilt vs used BMW M2 engine prices for your budget

items.question: What causes oil leaks on the BMW M2, and how much to fix?
items.answer: Ageing gaskets - particularly the valve cover, oil filter housing and oil pan - are the primary cause of oil leaks on the F87 M2, typically appearing from 50,000 miles onward .
keyPoints: Valve cover gasket leak (£200-£350) - burning oil smell most common sign
Oil filter housing gasket failure can contaminate serpentine belt
Oil pan gasket leak requires subframe lowering - higher labour cost
Ignoring leaks leads to low oil pressure and potential engine failure
G87 too new for gasket age-related issues
items.cta: Get BMW M2 oil leak repair quotes or replacement engine prices

items.question: What's the difference between reconditioned, rebuilt and used M2 engines?
items.answer: Rebuilt engines are fully disassembled and machined with new parts - the premium choice. Reconditioned means inspected and worn parts replaced to factory spec. Used engines are tested donor units with existing mileage .
keyPoints: Rebuilt: fully machined, new pistons/rings/bearings, upgraded failure points - £4,200-£9,500
Reconditioned: factory-spec rebuild with OEM parts - £3,800-£7,500
Used: tested donor engine, lowest cost but unknown history - £2,500-£5,500
Remanufactured: 'better than new' with blueprinted assembly - £5,000-£11,000
All types include 12-24 month warranty when sourced through our network
items.cta: Compare all BMW M2 engine condition types and prices side by side

items.question: Should I use a BMW dealer or independent specialist for M2 engine work?
items.answer: For routine maintenance either works, but for engine replacement or S55 crank hub work, a BMW specialist offers better value - typically 30-50% lower labour rates and proven experience with M engines .
keyPoints: Dealer labour: £150-£250/hour - OEM parts only
Specialist labour: £70-£120/hour - can fit upgraded parts like pinned hubs
Specialists often offer supply & fit packages with combined warranty
Many specialists have direct S55 crank hub fix experience - dealers may not
Our network connects you with vetted UK BMW M2 specialists
items.cta: Find a trusted BMW M2 engine specialist near you
